differences.title: 1. RENDERING STRATEGY
differences.leftDesc: Hydrates only interactive parts of UI on demand, reducing initial JS payload.
differences.rightDesc: Renders on server or client, offering flexibility for diverse application needs.

differences.title: 2. DEFAULT OUTPUT
differences.leftDesc: Generates static files by default, ideal for performance and SEO.
differences.rightDesc: Generates dynamic HTML on the server, good for personalized content.

differences.title: 3. CLIENT JAVASCRIPT
differences.leftDesc: Ships minimal or no JavaScript to the browser unless explicitly needed.
differences.rightDesc: Can ship more JavaScript for client-side interactivity and state management.

differences.title: 4. PERFORMANCE FOCUS
differences.leftDesc: Prioritizes speed and low bundle sizes for content-heavy sites.
differences.rightDesc: Optimizes for performance but balances with dynamic features.

differences.title: 5. USE CASE
differences.leftDesc: Blogs, marketing sites, documentation, e-commerce product pages.
differences.rightDesc: Dashboards, SaaS platforms, social networks, highly interactive UIs.

differences.title: 6. FRAMEWORK INTEGRATION
differences.leftDesc: Supports multiple UI frameworks (React, Vue, Svelte) or none.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily built around React and its ecosystem.

differences.title: 7. DEVELOPMENT STYLE
differences.leftDesc: Easier to get started, especially for static site generation.
differences.rightDesc: More advanced features and configurations for complex projects.
